content: GABA is an inhibitory neurotransmitter that slows down neuronal activity in the brain.
A study found that 100 milligrams of GABA reduced anxiety and helped people face their fears with less impact on stress response.
After taking 100 milligrams of GABA, volunteers had a brainwave pattern indicating lower effort focus and less anxiety compared to a placebo.
Volunteers who took GABA while crossing a suspension bridge had higher levels of IgA in their saliva compared to those who took a placebo.
Higher IgA levels in saliva suggest a stronger immune system and less activation of the stress response during fear-inducing situations.
Based on the study, GABA supplementation might help manage fear responses and prevent stress-induced immune system suppression.
